mechanism_narrative: A persistently elevated federal funds rate above recent history keeps discount rates high, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as long as rates remain elevated.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d return stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or high-rate periods show equal or higher forward returns than low-rate periods (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points relative to its 3-month prior level signals an emerging labor-market downturn (Sahm-rule-like trigger), historically preceding equity drawdowns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp relative to its value 3 months prior
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.7 with recent upward drift
failure_condition: In triggered episodes, 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the effect disappears (p > 0.10) once controlling for prior 60-day momentum.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in payroll employment growth and industrial production growth reflects broad-based real-economy strength that supports corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days; neither series alone reliably predicts returns without the other confirming.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS+INDPRO growth signal produces no higher Rank IC than either single-series growth signal alone over a 3-year rolling window (difference < 0.02), invalidating the interaction claim.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening regime (FEDFUNDS elevated and rising), a decline in housing starts signals interest-rate-sensitive demand destruction that spreads to broader consumer spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter; in easing regimes the same HOUST decline is not predictive.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.0% and rising over prior 6 months
failure_condition: HOUST decline predicts negative forward returns with similar magnitude and significance during easing regimes as during tightening regimes, or Rank IC in tightening regime subsample is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales reflects real-time consumer demand momentum that propagates quickly into short-term equity sentiment, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days whenever RSAFS growth is positive.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following positive RSAFS growth are not significantly different from returns following negative RSAFS growth (t-test p > 0.05) across a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unexpected acceleration in headline CPI above trend, occurring while the federal funds rate is already elevated, raises fears of further tightening and triggers a short-horizon negative equity reaction within 5 trading days as markets reprice terminal rate expectations.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL month-over-month annualized change exceeds 3% while FEDFUNDS > 4%
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation concern
failure_condition: Triggered CPI surprises coincide with positive or flat 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC across triggered episodes falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in real GDP growth and industrial production growth signals a durable supply-side expansion that supports sustained earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter; the effect requires both series to confirm since GDP alone is low-frequency and revision-prone.
failure_condition: Combined GDPC1-INDPRO growth signal shows no improvement in Rank IC over INDPRO alone (delta < 0.02) over any 3-year rolling window, or GDP revisions reverse the sign of the growth signal in over 30% of quarters.

mechanism_narrative: In a regime where unemployment is rising from cyclical lows (post-expansion peak), further upticks in UNRATE combined with payroll deceleration amplify recession fears and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days; the same UNRATE uptick during early-cycle recovery is not predictive because it reflects labor force re-entry rather than layoffs.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion peak transitioning to contraction
failure_condition: During late-cycle regimes, rising UNRATE fails to precede negative 20-day forward returns in at least 55% of instances, or the same relationship holds equally strongly in early-cycle recovery periods, contradicting the regime-conditional claim.
